executive_summary: 40-month temporal analysis reveals accelerated urban transformation. Built-up area increased 24% (+227 structures). Green cover declined 31.2% (340→234 hectares). Three confirmed encroachment zones occupy 30,500 sq.m of railway land. Drainage capacity reduced 35% in Zone D-1. Urban heat island effect +2.8°C. Revenue impact: ₹4.2 Cr annual land value at risk. Immediate enforcement required for Zone S-7 and Parcel A-17.
ai_findings: Built-up expansion: +227 structures (620→847), 24% growth over 40 months
Green cover: -106 hectares (340→234), 31.2% decline — exceeds critical threshold
Encroachment: 30,500 sq.m across 5 zones on designated railway land
Water bodies: stable count but 2.1% area reduction from sedimentation
Road network: +46 km expanded (110→156 segments), 42% growth
Drainage: 35% capacity loss in Zone D-1, 2 lines blocked

recommendations.action: Enforce eviction at Zone S-7 (22,000 sq.m commercial encroachment on railway land).
Field verify Parcel A-17 boundary violation (28m from track vs 30m threshold).
Clear Drainage D-1 before monsoon. Install temporary pumps at underpass Section 4.
Initiate compensatory afforestation for 150 hectares per Green Tribunal mandate.
Install permanent boundary markers at all 5 encroachment-prone zones.
Submit comprehensive compliance report to Railway Board.

infrastructure_warnings: 3 encroachment zones within 120m of active railway track — safety risk
Drainage capacity 35% below design — monsoon flooding risk at underpass Section 4
Signal tower sight-line partially obstructed by unauthorized construction
